hero_subtitle: SFTY connects multiple KYC and KYB providers into a unified compliance layer that structures onboarding workflows, preserves audit evidence, and supports risk-based customer review.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Compliance review slows without structured evidence
problem_description: Onboarding and KYC workflows often rely on disconnected provider checks that create gaps in audit trails and decision logging. This fragmentation makes it difficult for compliance teams to produce clear, defensible evidence for customer due diligence and regulator reviews.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects KYC and KYB provider checks, review actions, decision logs and evidence into a single workflow that creates a clear, auditable trail supporting compliance teams’ customer due diligence and regulator reviews.
workflow_title: From onboarding checks to audit evidence
workflow_description: SFTY structures KYC and KYB provider outputs into risk-based reviews including case creation, decision logging and evidence preservation for compliance audit trails.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond KYC outputs. One evidence layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects multiple KYC and KYB provider results into structured onboarding workflows, decision logs, audit evidence and reporting without replacing existing compliance tools.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs requiring structured provider orchestration, defensible decision records and enterprise-grade deployment for audit-ready KYC and KYB compliance workflows.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for KYC audit evidence?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects KYC and KYB provider outputs into structured onboarding reviews, decisions and audit evidence for clear, traceable customer due diligence workflows in regulated digital asset businesses.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle onboarding workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures onboarding by orchestrating checks from multiple providers, applying risk-based routing, opening cases, recording decisions and preserving evidence in aligned, auditable workflows.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve KYC audit trails?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves onboarding audit trails by recording decision logs, case histories, supporting evidence and timestamps to support clear review paths for compliance and regulatory inspection readiness.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate multiple KYC providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes, SFTY integrates multiple KYC and KYB providers into one platform, harmonizing their outputs to enable seamless onboarding and consistent compliance control across jurisdictions and products.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing KYC or KYB providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace KYC or KYB providers but sits above them, turning their outputs into structured workflows, decisions, compliance rules updates and audit-ready evidence.
final_title: Structure onboarding reviews with audit-ready evidence
final_description: Explore how SFTY connects KYC and KYB provider outputs into structured workflows with traceable decision and audit logs.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.

footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
